questions.question: What is power-sharing in a democracy?
questions.correct_answer: The distribution of political power among different groups, institutions, or levels of government
questions.explanation: Power-sharing ensures that no single person or group holds all the power.

questions.question: Which two countries are discussed in the chapter to explain power-sharing?
questions.explanation: These two countries show contrasting approaches to handling ethnic diversity.

questions.question: What is majoritarianism?
questions.correct_answer: The belief that the majority community should rule the country in whichever way it wants
questions.explanation: This approach can ignore the needs and rights of minority groups.

questions.question: Which language was declared the only official language in Sri Lanka in 1956?
questions.explanation: This policy marginalized the Tamil-speaking population.

questions.question: What was one major consequence of discrimination against Tamils in Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: The conflict arose due to political and cultural discrimination.

questions.question: How did Belgium address its ethnic conflict?
questions.correct_answer: By creating a power-sharing arrangement among linguistic communities
questions.explanation: Belgium amended its constitution several times to ensure equal representation.

questions.question: What is a civil war?
questions.correct_answer: A violent conflict between opposing groups within the same country
questions.explanation: Civil wars usually arise due to deep political or social divisions.

questions.question: What are prudential reasons for power-sharing?
questions.correct_answer: Reasons based on the benefits of reducing conflict and ensuring stability
questions.explanation: Power-sharing prevents violence and political instability.

questions.question: What are moral reasons for power-sharing?
questions.correct_answer: Reasons based on democratic values such as participation and equality
questions.explanation: Democracy requires that citizens share in decision-making.

questions.question: What is horizontal distribution of power?
questions.correct_answer: Power shared among different organs of government such as legislature, executive, and judiciary
questions.explanation: This system creates checks and balances between institutions.

questions.question: What is vertical distribution of power?
questions.correct_answer: Power shared among different levels of government such as central and state governments
questions.explanation: This is commonly seen in federal systems.

questions.question: What is a coalition government?
questions.correct_answer: A government formed by two or more political parties working together
questions.explanation: Coalitions share power when no single party wins a majority.

notes.title: Power-sharing
notes.content: Power-sharing means distributing political power among different institutions, groups, or levels of government to maintain democratic balance.

notes.title: Majoritarianism
notes.content: Majoritarianism occurs when the majority community dominates political decisions and ignores minority interests.

notes.title: Belgium Model
notes.content: Belgium adopted a complex power-sharing arrangement including equal representation of language groups and community governments.

notes.title: Reasons for Power-sharing
notes.content: Power-sharing is supported by prudential reasons (stability and conflict reduction) and moral reasons (democratic participation).

notes.title: Forms of Power-sharing
notes.content: Power-sharing can occur among organs of government, levels of government, social groups, and political parties.

facts.fact: Belgium amended its constitution multiple times between 1970 and 1993 to create power-sharing arrangements.
Civil war in Sri Lanka lasted for decades and ended in 2009.
Power-sharing is considered the spirit of democracy because it allows citizens to participate in governance.
